MOTION TO STRIKE Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-27-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTS WALDEK KACZMARSKI, DEEPA BALGI, HAMMEL, GREEN AND ABRAHAMSON, INC.'S MOTION TO STRIKE Amended COMPLAINT.

Defendant's motion to strike the punitive damages allegations is denied.

"Any party, within the time allowed to respond to a pleading may serve and file a notice of motion to strike the whole or any part thereof..." (CCP 435.) "The court may, upon a motion made pursuant to Section 435, or at any time in its discretion, and upon terms it deems proper: [para] (a) Strike out any irrelevant, false, or improper matter inserted in any pleading. (b) Strike out all or any part of any pleading not drawn or filed in conformity with the laws of this state, a court rule, or an order of the court." (CCP 436.) "In the construction of a pleading, for the purpose of determining its effect, its allegations must be liberally construed, with a view to substantial justice between the parties." (CCP 452.)

Defendants seek to strike Plaintiff's punitive damages allegations. "In an action for the breach of an obligation not arising from contract, where it is proven by clear and convincing evidence that the defendant has been guilty of oppression, fraud, or malice, the plaintiff, in addition to the actual damages, may recover damages for the sake of example and by way of punishing the defendant." (Civil Code section 3294.) "The ultimate facts showing entitlement to such relief must be pled by a plaintiff." (Clauson v Superior Court (1998) 67 Cal.App.4th 1253, 1255.) This is a pleading standard. (Grieves v. Superior Court (1984) 157 Cal.App.3d 159, 166.) "Not only must there be circumstances of oppression, fraud or malice, but facts must be alleged in the pleading to support such a claim." (Id.) "In passing on the correctness of a ruling on a motion to strike, judges read allegations of a pleading subject to a motion to strike as a whole, all parts in their context, and assume their truth." (Clauson, 67 Cal. App. 4th at 1255.)

Plaintiff alleges sufficient ultimate facts to support the claim.

Plaintiff's request for judicial notice is denied. The news article profiling Ms. Balgi is not a proper subject for judicial notice under Evidence code section 452(h).

Counsel for Plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn; Clerk: M. Macadangdang; Not Reported. (302/JMQ)
